notes.note: Isidorus, an agent reporting to Cicero on the sale of his properties; the failure to sell the estates threatened the cash Cicero needed in his stranded state.
Cicero's farm at Frusino in the Volscian hills of Latium. With his finances in ruin, retaining and enjoying this modest estate would be a welcome consolation.

Q. Pilius Celer, a connection of Atticus (whose wife Pilia was Celer's relation), here serving as the trusted courier who will relay verbally what Cicero dares not commit to writing under Caesarian watch.
